title: How IAM evaluates a request: deny, boundary, allow
description: Explains the order of explicit deny, SCPs, permission boundaries, session policies, and identity vs resource policies, and why an Allow can still be denied.
body: Use when reasoning about why an IAM request is allowed or denied. Do not assume an identity policy Allow is sufficient. Actions: apply the evaluation order — an explicit Deny anywhere wins; the request must be allowed within the SCP, the permission boundary, the session policy (if assumed role), and the identity policy; for cross-account or S3/KMS the resource policy must also allow. Verify with simulate-principal-policy. Validation signals include the simulator's matched statements explaining each Allow/Deny and CloudTrail's errorCode matching the predicted gate. Risk boundary: widening any boundary or SCP to pass a check is a privilege change requiring human review.
